returns._0: the state of the consensus client
the last updated time of the consensus client
the most recent authority set ID (epoch) for which a consensus proof has been submitted

deleteStateMachineCommitment((uint256,ui.details: Delete the state commitment at given state height.

dispatch((bytes,bytes,bytes,uint64,uint2.details: Dispatch a POST request to Hyperbridge

dispatch((bytes,uint64,bytes[],uint64,ui.details: Dispatch a GET request to Hyperbridge

dispatchIncoming(((bytes,bytes,uint64,by.details: Dispatch an incoming get response to source app

dispatchIncoming((bytes,bytes,uint64,byt.details: Dispatch an incoming request to destination app

dispatchTimeOut(((bytes,bytes,uint64,byt.details: Dispatch an incoming post timeout to source app

params.meta: - fee metadata for the original request
params.timeout: - timed-out post request bundled with the relayer that submitted the timeout proof

dispatchTimeOut(((bytes,bytes,uint64,byt.details: Dispatch an incoming get timeout to source app

params.meta: - fee metadata for the original request
params.timeout: - timed-out get request bundled with the relayer that submitted the timeout proof

feeToken().details: Returns the address of the ERC-20 fee token contract configured for this state machine.

returns._0: feeToken - The ERC20 contract address for fees.

fundRequest(bytes32,uint256).details: Increase the relayer fee for a previously dispatched request. This is provided for use only on pending requests, such that when they timeout, the user can recover the entire relayer fee.

returns._0: the state machine identifier for the connected hyperbridge instance
the latest state machine height for the given stateMachineId. If it returns 0, the state machine is unsupported.

nonce().details: Returns the nonce immediately available for requests

recordEpoch(uint256,address).details: Record the relayer that first submitted a consensus proof for a new authority set epoch. Only callable by the configured handler. The recorded relayer is the one passed in by the handler at the call site; the host trusts the handler to identify the relayer.

params.authoritySetId: the new authority set / epoch ID
params.relayer: the relayer that delivered the consensus proof

relayerOf(uint256).details: Returns the relayer that first submitted the consensus proof for the given epoch.

returns._0: the relayer address, or address(0) if not set
existence status of an outgoing request commitment

params.commitment: - commitment to the request of the response

setFrozenState(uint8).details: set the new frozen state of the host, only the admin or handler can call this.

returns._0: the state machine commitment update time at `height`

uniswapV2Router().details: Returns the address for the Uniswap V2 Router implementation used for swaps

returns._0: routerAddress - The address to the in-use RouterV02 implementation

vetoes(uint256,uint256).details: Returns the fisherman responsible for vetoing the given state machine height.

dispatch((bytes,bytes,bytes,uint64,uint2.notice: Payment for the request can be made with either the native token or the IHost.feeToken. If native tokens are supplied, it will perform a swap under the hood using the local uniswap router. Will revert if enough native tokens are not provided. If no native tokens are provided then it will try to collect payment from the calling contract in the IHost.feeToken.

dispatch((bytes,uint64,bytes[],uint64,ui.notice: Payment for the request can be made with either the native token or the IHost.feeToken. If native tokens are supplied, it will perform a swap under the hood using the local uniswap router. Will revert if enough native tokens are not provided. If no native tokens are provided then it will try to collect payment from the calling contract in the IHost.feeToken.

feeToken().notice: Hyperbridge collects it's dispatch fees in the provided token denomination. This will typically be in stablecoins.

fundRequest(bytes32,uint256).notice: Payment can be made with either the native token or the IHost.feeToken. If native tokens are supplied, it will perform a swap under the hood using the local uniswap router. Will revert if enough native tokens are not provided. If no native tokens are provided then it will try to collect payment from the calling contract in the IHost.feeToken. If called on an already delivered request, these funds will be seen as a donation to the hyperbridge protocol.
